Output:
Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
 261: function gitCommonDir(cwd: string): string {
 262:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--git-common-dir'], {
 263:     cwd,
 264:   }).trim()
 265: 
 266:   if (!output) {
 267:     throw new Error(`Unable to resolve git common-dir for: ${cwd}`)
 268:   }
 269: 
 270:   return isAbsolute(output) ? resolve(output) : resolve(cwd, output)
 271: }
 272: 
 273: function resolveTrackingRemote(projectPath: string): string | null {
 274:   const upstream = execCommand(
 275:     'git',
 276:     ['rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', '--symbolic-full-name', '@{u}'],
 277:     { cwd: projectPath, allowFailure: true },
 278:   ).trim()
 279:   if (!upstream) {
 280:     return null
 281:   }
 282: 
 283:   const remotes = listRemoteNames(projectPath)
 284:     .sort((left, right) => right.length - left.length)
 285:   return remotes.find(remote => upstream === remote || upstream.startsWith(`${remote}/`)) ?? null
 286: }
 287: 
 288: function pushToResolvedRemote(
 289:   projectPath: string,
 290:   target: PushRemoteStatus & { remote: string; pushUrl: string; transport: PushTransport },
 291:   pushArgs: string[],
 292: ): BranchPushStatus {
 293:   try {
 294:     git(projectPath, ...pushArgs)
 295:     return {
 296:       remote: target.remote,
 297:       pushUrl: target.pushUrl,
 298:       transport: target.transport,
 299:       usedFallback: false,
 300:     }
 301:   } catch (primaryError: unknown) {
 302:     if (shouldTryFallbackTransport(primaryError, target.fallbackUrls)) {
 303:       let lastFallbackFailure: { url: string; error: unknown } | null = null
 304:       for (const fallbackUrl of target.fallbackUrls) {
 305:         try {
 306:           execCommand(
 307:             'git',
 308:             ['-c', `remote.${target.remote}.pushurl=${fallbackUrl}`, ...pushArgs],
 309:             { cwd: projectPath },
 310:           )
 311:           return {
 312:             remote: target.remote,
 313:             pushUrl: fallbackUrl,
 314:             transport: detectPushTransport(fallbackUrl),
 315:             usedFallback: true,
 316:           }
 317:         } catch (fallbackError: unknown) {
 318:           lastFallbackFailure = {
 319:             url: fallbackUrl,
 320:             error: fallbackError,
 321:           }
 322:         }
 323:       }
 324:       if (lastFallbackFailure !== null) {
 325:         throw new Error(
 326:           formatPushFailure(
 327:             target.remote,
 328:             target.pushUrl,
 329:             primaryError,
 330:             lastFallbackFailure.url,
 331:             lastFallbackFailure.error,
 332:           ),
 333:         )
 334:       }
 335:     }
 336: 
 337:     throw new Error(formatPushFailure(target.remote, target.pushUrl, primaryError))
 338:   }
 339: }
 340: 
 341: function detectPushTransport(url: string): PushTransport {
 342:   const normalized = url.trim().toLowerCase()
 343:   if (normalized.startsWith('https://')) {
 344:     return 'https'
 345:   }
 346:   if (normalized.startsWith('ssh://') || normalized.startsWith('git@')) {
 347:     return 'ssh'
 348:   }
 349:   return 'other'
 350: }
 351: 
 352: function collectFallbackPushUrls(pushUrl: string): string[] {
 353:   const repo = parseGitHubRepo(pushUrl)
 354:   if (repo === null) {
 355:     return []
 356:   }
 357: 
 358:   const candidates = alternateTransportUrls(repo)
 359: 
 360:   const seen = new Set<string>()
 361:   const normalizedPrimary = normalizeComparableUrl(pushUrl)
 362:   const fallbackUrls: string[] = []
 363: 
 364:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
 365:     const trimmed = candidate.trim()
 366:     if (!trimmed) continue
 367: 
 368:     const normalizedCandidate = normalizeComparableUrl(trimmed)
 369:     if (normalizedCandidate === normalizedPrimary || seen.has(normalizedCandidate)) {
 370:       continue
 371:     }
 372: 
 373:     seen.add(normalizedCandidate)
 374:     fallbackUrls.push(trimmed)
 375:   }
 376: 
 377:   return fallbackUrls
 378: }
 379: 
 380: function alternateTransportUrls(repo: { owner: string; repo: string }): string[] {
 381:   const httpsUrl = `https://github.com/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
 382:   const sshUrl = `ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/${repo.owner}/${repo.repo}.git`
 383: 
 384:   return [httpsUrl, sshUrl]
 385: }
 386: 
 387: function parseGitHubRepo(url: string): { owner: string; repo: string } | null {
 388:   const trimmed = url.trim()
 389:   if (!trimmed) {
 390:     return null
 391:   }
 392: 
 393:   const httpsMatch = trimmed.match(/^https:\/\/github\.com\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
 394:   if (httpsMatch) {
 395:     return {
 396:       owner: httpsMatch[1]!,
 397:       repo: httpsMatch[2]!,
 398:     }
 399:   }
 400: 
 401:   const scpMatch = trimmed.match(/^git@github\.com:([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?$/i)
 402:   if (scpMatch) {
 403:     return {
 404:       owner: scpMatch[1]!,
 405:       repo: scpMatch[2]!,
 406:     }
 407:   }
 408: 
 409:   const sshMatch = trimmed.match(/^ssh:\/\/git@(?:github\.com|ssh\.github\.com(?::443)?)\/([^/\s]+)\/([^/\s]+?)(?:\.git)?\/?$/i)
 410:   if (sshMatch) {
 411:     return {
 412:       owner: sshMatch[1]!,
 413:       repo: sshMatch[2]!,
 414:     }
 415:   }
 416: 
 417:   return null
 418: }
 419: 
 420: function shouldTryFallbackTransport(error: unknown, fallbackUrls: readonly string[]): boolean {
 421:   if (fallbackUrls.length === 0) {
 422:     return false
 423:   }
 424: 
 425:   const message = collectErrorText(error).toLowerCase()
 426:   return [
 427:     'host key verification failed',
 428:     'permission denied (publickey)',
 429:     'could not read from remote repository',
 430:     'authentication failed',
 431:     'repository not found',
 432:     'could not resolve host',
 433:     'unable to access',
 434:     'connection timed out',
 435:     'connection refused',
 436:     'ssh:',
 437:   ].some(fragment => message.includes(fragment))
 438: }
 439: 
 440: function formatPushFailure(
 441:   remote: string,
 442:   pushUrl: string,
 443:   primaryError: unknown,
 444:   fallbackUrl?: string,
 445:   fallbackError?: unknown,
 446: ): string {
 447:   const lines = [
 448:     `Unable to push branch to remote '${remote}'.`,
 449:     `Primary push URL: ${redactRemoteUrl(pushUrl)}`,
 450:     `Primary error: ${summarizeError(primaryError)}`,
 451:   ]
 452: 
 453:   if (fallbackUrl) {
 454:     lines.push(`Fallback push URL: ${redactRemoteUrl(fallbackUrl)}`)
 455:     if (fallbackError) {
 456:       lines.push(`Fallback error: ${summarizeError(fallbackError)}`)
 457:     }
 458:   }
 459: 
 460:   return lines.join('\n')
 461: }
 462: 
 463: function summarizeError(error: unknown): string {
 464:   const lines = redactRemoteUrlsInText(collectErrorText(error))
 465:     .split(/\r?\n/)
 466:     .map(line => line.trim())
 467:     .filter(Boolean)
 468: 
 469:   const preferred = lines.find(line => !line.startsWith('Command failed:'))
 470:   return preferred ?? lines[0] ?? String(error)
 471: }
 472: 
 473: function collectErrorText(error: unknown): string {
 474:   if (error instanceof Error) {
 475:     const parts = [error.message]
 476:     const extra = error as {
 477:       readonly stdout?: string | Buffer
 478:       readonly stderr?: string | Buffer
 479:     }
 480:     if (extra.stdout !== undefined) {
 481:       parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stdout))
 482:     }
 483:     if (extra.stderr !== undefined) {
 484:       parts.push(bufferToString(extra.stderr))
 485:     }
 486:     return parts.filter(Boolean).join('\n')
 487:   }
 488: 
 489:   return String(error)
 490: }
 491: 
 492: function bufferToString(value: string | Buffer): string {
 493:   return typeof value === 'string' ? value : value.toString('utf-8')
 494: }
 495: 
 496: function redactRemoteUrlsInText(value: string): string {
 497:   return value.replace(/(https?:\/\/)([^/\s@]+)@/gi, '$1***@')
 498: }
 499: 
 500: function normalizeComparableUrl(value: string): string {
 501:   return value.trim().replace(/\/+$/, '').toLowerCase()
 502: }
 503: 
 504: function listRemoteNames(projectPath: string): string[] {
 505:   return git(projectPath, 'remote')
 506:     .split(/\r?\n/)
 507:     .map(remote => remote.trim())
 508:     .filter(Boolean)
 509: }
 510: 
 511: function branchExists(repoRoot: string, branch: string): boolean {
 512:   const output = execCommand('git', ['rev-parse', '--verify', `refs/heads/${branch}`], {
 513:     cwd: repoRoot,
 514:     allowFailure: true,
 515:   })
 516:   return output.trim().length > 0
 517: }
 518: 
 519: function samePath(left: string, right: string): boolean {
 520:   return normalizePath(left) === normalizePath(right)

 331:   }
 332: 
 333:   if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
 334:     logger.warn(`[${projectName}] Push failed, retrying once...`)
 335:     if (!tryPush(projectPath, projectName)) {
 336:       logger.error(`[${projectName}] Push failed after retry.`)
 337:       writeAlertWithThreshold(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed', 'Push failed after retry.')
 338:       return
 339:     }
 340:   }
 341: 
 342:   resetFailureCount(toPosixPath(projectPath), 'push_failed')
 343: }
 344: 
 345: export function tryPush(projectPath: string, projectName: string): boolean {
 346:   try {
 347:     const branch = currentBranch(projectPath)
 348:     if (hasTrackingUpstream(projectPath)) {
 349:       const pushed = pushBranchToRemote(projectPath, branch)
 350:       if (pushed.remote === null) {
 351:         return false
 352:       }
 353:       if (pushed.usedFallback && pushed.pushUrl !== null) {
 354:         getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully via fallback ${redactRemoteUrl(pushed.pushUrl)}.`)
 355:       }
 356:     } else {
 357:       const upstream = ensureBranchUpstream(projectPath, branch)
 358:       if (upstream.remote === null) {
 359:         return false
 360:       }
 361:       const fallbackNote = upstream.usedFallback && upstream.pushUrl !== null
 362:         ? ` via fallback ${redactRemoteUrl(upstream.pushUrl)}`
 363:         : ''
 364:       getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Configured upstream ${upstream.remote}/${branch}${fallbackNote}.`)
 365:     }
 366:     getLogger().info(`[${projectName}] Pushed successfully.`)
 367:     return true
 368:   } catch {
 369:     return false
 370:   }
 371: }
 372: 
 373: export function currentBranch(projectPath: string): string {
 374:   return git(projectPath, 'rev-parse', '--abbrev-ref', 'HEAD').trim()
 375: }

/**
 * Subprocess execution wrapper for git and other commands.
 * Port of heartbeat.py _git() and daemon subprocess calls.
 */

import { execFileSync } from 'node:child_process'

export function execCommand(
  command: string,
  args: readonly string[],
  options?: { cwd?: string; timeout?: number; allowFailure?: boolean },
): string {
  const cwd = options?.cwd
  const timeout = options?.timeout ?? 120_000
  const allowFailure = options?.allowFailure ?? false

  try {
    const stdout = execFileSync(command, [...args], {
      cwd,
      timeout,
      encoding: 'utf-8',
      stdio: ['pipe', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
    })
    return stdout
  } catch (err: unknown) {
    if (allowFailure) {
      const e = err as { stdout?: string }
      return typeof e.stdout === 'string' ? e.stdout : ''
    }
    throw err
  }
}

export function git(cwd: string, ...args: string[]): string {
  const allowFailure = args[0] === 'status' || args[0] === 'remote'
  return execCommand('git', args, { cwd, allowFailure })
}

我在顺手验证一个很细但挺关键的点：它的 fallback 是通过 git -c remote.<name>.pushurl=... 注入的，如果 remote 名带 /，这种配置写法得先确认 Git 真能吃。

  78:   it('creates the memorytree branch automatically when the dedicated worktree does not exist yet', async () => {
  79:     const freshMemoryPath = join(sandboxRoot, 'fresh-memory-worktree')
  80:     const gitMock = vi.fn((cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
  81:       if (cwd === developmentPath && args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--show-toplevel') {
  82:         return `${developmentPath}\n`
  83:       }
  84:       if (
  85:         cwd === developmentPath
  86:         && args[0] === 'worktree'
  87:         && args[1] === 'add'
  88:         && args[2] === '-b'
  89:         && args[3] === 'memorytree'
  90:         && args[4] === freshMemoryPath
  91:         && args[5] === 'HEAD'
  92:       ) {
  93:         return ''
  94:       }
  95:       throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${cwd} ${args.join(' ')}`)
  96:     })
  97: 
  98:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
  99:       git: gitMock,
 100:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 101:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--git-common-dir' && options?.cwd === developmentPath) {
 102:           return `${commonDir}\n`
 103:         }
 104:         if (args[0] === 'check-ref-format' && args[1] === '--branch' && args[2] === 'memorytree') {
 105:           return 'memorytree\n'
 106:         }
 107:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--verify') {
 108:           return ''
 109:         }
 110:         throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 111:       },
 112:     }))
 113: 
 114:     const { ensureProjectWorktree } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
 115: 
 116:     const result = ensureProjectWorktree({
 117:       development_path: developmentPath,
 118:       memory_path: freshMemoryPath,
 119:       memory_branch: 'memorytree',
 120:     } as never)
 121: 
 122:     expect(result).toEqual({
 123:       branch: 'memorytree',
 124:       created: true,
 125:     })
 126:     expect(gitMock).toHaveBeenCalledWith(
 127:       developmentPath,
 128:       'worktree',
 129:       'add',
 130:       '-b',
 131:       'memorytree',
 132:       freshMemoryPath,
 133:       'HEAD',
 134:     )
 135:   })
 136: })

 138: describe('ensureBranchUpstream', () => {
 139:   it('falls back from GitHub SSH push URLs to HTTPS when the SSH push fails', async () => {
 140:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
 141:       git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 142:         if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
 143:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
 144:         }
 145:         if (args[0] === 'remote') {
 146:           return 'origin\n'
 147:         }
 148:         if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
 149:           const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
 150:           Object.assign(error, {
 151:             stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
 152:           })
 153:           throw error
 154:         }
 155:         throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 156:       },
 157:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 158:         if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
 159:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
 160:         }
 161:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
 162:           return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
 163:         }
 164:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
 165:           return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
 166:         }
 167:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
 168:           return ''
 169:         }
 170:         if (
 171:           args[0] === '-c'
 172:           && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
 173:           && args[2] === 'push'
 174:           && args[3] === '-u'
 175:           && args[4] === 'origin'
 176:           && args[5] === 'memorytree'
 177:         ) {
 178:           return ''
 179:         }
 180:         throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 181:       },
 182:     }))
 183: 
 184:     const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
 185: 
 186:     const result = ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
 187: 
 188:     expect(result).toEqual({
 189:       remote: 'origin',
 190:       created: true,
 191:       pushUrl: 'https://github.com/example/repo.git',
 192:       transport: 'https',
 193:       usedFallback: true,
 194:     })
 195:   })
 196: 
 197:   it('does not use a different fetch mirror as the fallback push target', async () => {
 198:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
 199:       git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 200:         if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
 201:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
 202:         }
 203:         if (args[0] === 'remote') {
 204:           return 'origin\n'
 205:         }
 206:         if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
 207:           const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
 208:           Object.assign(error, {
 209:             stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
 210:           })
 211:           throw error
 212:         }
 213:         throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 214:       },
 215:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 216:         if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
 217:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
 218:         }
 219:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
 220:           return 'https://github.com/example/read-mirror.git\n'
 221:         }
 222:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
 223:           return 'git@github.com:example/write-repo.git\n'
 224:         }
 225:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
 226:           return ''
 227:         }
 228:         if (
 229:           args[0] === '-c'
 230:           && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/write-repo.git'
 231:           && args[2] === 'push'
 232:           && args[3] === '-u'
 233:           && args[4] === 'origin'
 234:           && args[5] === 'memorytree'
 235:         ) {
 236:           return ''
 237:         }
 238:         if (args[0] === '-c') {
 239:           throw new Error(`Unexpected fallback push target: ${args[1]}`)
 240:         }
 241:         throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 242:       },
 243:     }))
 244: 
 245:     const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
 246: 
 247:     const result = ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')

 251:       created: true,

 254:       usedFallback: true,
 255:     })
 256:   })
 257: 
 258:   it('reports both the primary and fallback push failures when fallback also fails', async () => {
 259:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
 260:       git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 261:         if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
 262:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
 263:         }
 264:         if (args[0] === 'remote') {
 265:           return 'origin\n'
 266:         }
 267:         if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
 268:           const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
 269:           Object.assign(error, {
 270:             stderr: 'Host key verification failed.\nfatal: Could not read from remote repository.\n',
 271:           })
 272:           throw error
 273:         }
 274:         throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 275:       },
 276:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 277:         if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
 278:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
 279:         }
 280:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
 281:           return 'https://github.com/example/repo.git\n'
 282:         }
 283:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
 284:           return 'ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git\n'
 285:         }
 286:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
 287:           return ''
 288:         }
 289:         if (
 290:           args[0] === '-c'
 291:           && args[1] === 'remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git'
 292:           && args[2] === 'push'
 293:           && args[3] === '-u'
 294:           && args[4] === 'origin'
 295:           && args[5] === 'memorytree'
 296:         ) {
 297:           const error = new Error('Command failed: git -c remote.origin.pushurl=https://github.com/example/repo.git push -u origin memorytree')
 298:           Object.assign(error, {
 299:             stderr: 'Authentication failed for https://github.com/example/repo.git\n',
 300:           })
 301:           throw error
 302:         }
 303:         throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 304:       },
 305:     }))
 306: 
 307:     const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
 308: 
 309:     let thrown: unknown = null
 310:     try {
 311:       ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
 312:     } catch (error: unknown) {
 313:       thrown = error
 314:     }
 315: 
 316:     expect(thrown).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
 317:     expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Primary push URL: ssh://git@ssh.github.com:443/example/repo.git')
 318:     expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Fallback push URL: https://github.com/example/repo.git')
 319:     expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Host key verification failed.')
 320:     expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Authentication failed for https://github.com/example/repo.git')
 321:   })
 322: 
 323:   it('redacts credentials from failure details', async () => {
 324:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
 325:       git: (cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 326:         if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
 327:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
 328:         }
 329:         if (args[0] === 'remote') {
 330:           return 'origin\n'
 331:         }
 332:         if (args[0] === 'push' && args[1] === '-u' && args[2] === 'origin' && args[3] === 'memorytree') {
 333:           const error = new Error('Command failed: git push -u origin memorytree')
 334:           Object.assign(error, {
 335:             stderr: 'Authentication failed for https://x-access-token:super-secret-token@example.internal/repo.git\n',
 336:           })
 337:           throw error
 338:         }
 339:         throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 340:       },
 341:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 342:         if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
 343:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
 344:         }
 345:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {
 346:           return 'https://x-access-token:super-secret-token@example.internal/repo.git\n'
 347:         }
 348:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === '--push' && args[3] === 'origin') {
 349:           return 'https://x-access-token:super-secret-token@example.internal/repo.git\n'
 350:         }
 351:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
 352:           return ''
 353:         }
 354:         throw new Error(`Unexpected execCommand call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 355:       },
 356:     }))
 357: 
 358:     const { ensureBranchUpstream } = await import('../../src/heartbeat/worktree.js')
 359: 
 360:     let thrown: unknown = null
 361:     try {
 362:       ensureBranchUpstream(developmentPath, 'memorytree')
 363:     } catch (error: unknown) {
 364:       thrown = error
 365:     }
 366: 
 367:     expect(thrown).toBeInstanceOf(Error)
 368:     expect((thrown as Error).message).toContain('Primary push URL: https://***@example.internal/repo.git')
 369:     expect((thrown as Error).message).not.toContain('super-secret-token')
 370:   })
 371: })

 373: describe('pushBranchToRemote', () => {
 374:   it('preserves the existing tracking upstream semantics by using plain git push', async () => {
 375:     const gitMock = vi.fn((cwd: string, ...args: string[]) => {
 376:       if (cwd !== developmentPath) {
 377:         throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${cwd}`)
 378:       }
 379:       if (args[0] === 'push') {
 380:         return ''
 381:       }
 382:       if (args[0] === 'remote') {
 383:         return 'origin\n'
 384:       }
 385:       throw new Error(`Unexpected git call: ${args.join(' ')}`)
 386:     })
 387: 
 388:     vi.doMock('../../src/utils/exec.js', () => ({
 389:       git: gitMock,
 390:       execCommand: (_command: string, args: string[], options?: { cwd?: string; allowFailure?: boolean }) => {
 391:         if (options?.cwd !== developmentPath) {
 392:           throw new Error(`Unexpected cwd: ${options?.cwd}`)
 393:         }
 394:         if (args[0] === 'rev-parse' && args[1] === '--abbrev-ref' && args[2] === '--symbolic-full-name') {
 395:           return 'origin/memorytree-remote\n'
 396:         }
 397:         if (args[0] === 'remote' && args[1] === 'get-url' && args[2] === 'origin') {

 345:   it('redacts credentials in CLI push URL output', async () => {
 346:     const savedConfigs: unknown[] = []
 347:     const upserted = {

assistant #333 final_answer 04:56:13

1. 代码审查

- 未发现阻塞性问题。我看了 [PR #39](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39) 里最核心的三块：upstream 建立、已跟踪分支的 push、以及 push URL 脱敏，逻辑是自洽的，新增回归测试也覆盖到了最容易出问题的几条路径。
- 非阻塞的边界点有一个：这次 fallback 明显是按 GitHub 形态写的，适合 github.com 和 ssh.github.com:443，对 GitHub Enterprise 或少见的 SSH URL 变体不算完整覆盖。不过对这个仓库当前场景，我不把它当成 blocker。

2. 合并判断

- 从代码角度看，可以合并。
- 从分支状态看，也没有冲突面。GitHub 现在给这个 PR 的状态是 MERGEABLE，而且它是从当前 main 直接分出来的：main 这边 0 个新提交，PR 自己 2 个提交，不需要先 rebase。
- 我在临时 worktree 里实际跑了这次改动直接相关的验证：typecheck、build、以及相关单测都过了。

现在唯一要注意的是流程层面：
- GitHub 把这个 PR 标成 UNSTABLE，因为 [PR 的 E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919) 是红的。
- 但我对了下当前 main 的最近一次 [E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个失败：git rm --cached AGENTS.md 找不到文件。
- 所以这更像仓库当前已经存在的 E2E 问题，不像是 #39 新引入的回归。

结论很直接：
- 如果你问“这 PR 本身值不值得合、会不会把主干搞坏”，我的答案是可以合。
- 如果你问“现在能不能在流程上直接点 merge”，要看仓库保护规则；如果要求 checks 全绿，那得先处理这个 E2E 红灯，哪怕它不是这个 PR 造成的。

Show task summary

1. 代码审查

- 未发现阻塞性问题。我看了 [PR #39](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39) 里最核心的三块：upstream 建立、已跟踪分支的 push、以及 push URL 脱敏，逻辑是自洽的，新增回归测试也覆盖到了最容易出问题的几条路径。
- 非阻塞的边界点有一个：这次 fallback 明显是按 GitHub 形态写的，适合 github.com 和 ssh.github.com:443，对 GitHub Enterprise 或少见的 SSH URL 变体不算完整覆盖。不过对这个仓库当前场景，我不把它当成 blocker。

2. 合并判断

- 从代码角度看，可以合并。
- 从分支状态看，也没有冲突面。GitHub 现在给这个 PR 的状态是 MERGEABLE，而且它是从当前 main 直接分出来的：main 这边 0 个新提交，PR 自己 2 个提交，不需要先 rebase。
- 我在临时 worktree 里实际跑了这次改动直接相关的验证：typecheck、build、以及相关单测都过了。

现在唯一要注意的是流程层面：
- GitHub 把这个 PR 标成 UNSTABLE，因为 [PR 的 E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23525333919) 是红的。
- 但我对了下当前 main 的最近一次 [E2E](https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/actions/runs/23524377763)，是同一个失败：git rm --cached AGENTS.md 找不到文件。
- 所以这更像仓库当前已经存在的 E2E 问题，不像是 #39 新引入的回归。

结论很直接：
- 如果你问“这 PR 本身值不值得合、会不会把主干搞坏”，我的答案是可以合。
- 如果你问“现在能不能在流程上直接点 merge”，要看仓库保护规则；如果要求 checks 全绿，那得先处理这个 E2E 红灯，哪怕它不是这个 PR 造成的。

    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

assistant #360 commentary 05:02:41

根因已经看到了：失败不是 #39 造成的，而是这条 E2E 自己强行执行 git rm --cached AGENTS.md，但升级后的测试仓库里并不一定有 AGENTS.md 被跟踪。接下来我会把这个 helper 改成“文件存在才 de-track”，让它符合实际仓库状态。

  14: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  15: 
  16: export const MEMORY_DIRS: readonly string[] = [
  17:   'Memory/01_goals',
  18:   'Memory/02_todos',
  19:   'Memory/03_chat_logs',
  20:   'Memory/04_knowledge',
  21:   'Memory/05_archive',
  22: ]
  23: 
  24: export const MANAGED_GITIGNORE_ENTRIES: readonly string[] = [
  25:   'AGENTS.md',
  26:   'Memory/**',
  27:   'memory/**',
  28: ] as const
  29: 
  30: export interface GitignoreEnsureResult {
  31:   readonly path: string
  32:   readonly added: readonly string[]
  33:   readonly changed: boolean
  34: }
  35: 
  36: export const CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS: readonly string[] = [
  37:   'CONTRIBUTING',
  38:   'CONTRIBUTING.md',
  39: ]
  40: 
  41: export const ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS: readonly string[] = [
  42:   'CODEOWNERS',
  43:   '.github/CODEOWNERS',
  44:   '.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md',
  45:   '.github/pull_request_template.md',
  46:   '.commitlintrc',
  47:   '.commitlintrc.json',
  48:   '.commitlintrc.yaml',
  49:   '.commitlintrc.yml',
  50:   '.commitlintrc.js',
  51:   '.commitlintrc.cjs',
  52:   'commitlint.config.js',
  53:   'commitlint.config.cjs',
  54:   'commitlint.config.mjs',
  55:   '.pre-commit-config.yaml',
  56:   '.pre-commit-config.yml',
  57: ]
  58: 
  59: const POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS: readonly RegExp[] = [
  60:   /\bpull request\b/i, /\bmerge request\b/i, /\bpr\b/i,
  61:   /\bbranch(?:es)?\b/i, /\bprotected branch(?:es)?\b/i,
  62:   /\bcommit(?: message)?\b/i, /\bconventional commit(?:s)?\b/i,
  63:   /\breview(?:er|ers)?\b/i, /\bapproval(?:s)?\b/i,
  64:   /\bci\b/i, /\bpipeline(?:s)?\b/i, /\bauto-merge\b/i,
  65:   /\bpre-commit\b/i, /\bcodeowners\b/i,
  66:   /鎷夊彇璇锋眰/, /鍚堝苟璇锋眰/, /鍒嗘敮/, /鎻愪氦淇℃伅/, /鎻愪氦鏍囬/,
  67:   /璇勫/, /瀹℃壒/, /娴佹按绾?, /妫€鏌?, /鑷姩鍚堝苟/, /鎻愪氦瑙勮寖/,
  68: ]
  69: 
  70: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  71: // Types
  72: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  73: 
  74: export interface ScaffoldPaths {
  75:   readonly root: string
  76:   readonly goalDir: string
  77:   readonly todoDir: string
  78:   readonly chatDir: string
  79:   readonly goalPath: string
  80:   readonly todoPath: string
  81:   readonly chatPath: string
  82:   readonly goalVersion: string
  83:   readonly todoVersion: string
  84:   readonly previousVersion: string
  85:   readonly dateLabel: string
  86:   readonly timeLabel: string
  87: }
  88: 
  89: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  90: // DateTime helper
  91: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  92: 
  93: export function buildDatetime(dateValue: string, timeValue: string): Date {
  94:   if (dateValue && timeValue) {
  95:     return new Date(`${dateValue}T${timeValue}:00`)
  96:   }
  97:   if (dateValue) {
  98:     return new Date(`${dateValue}T00:00:00`)
  99:   }
 100:   if (timeValue) {
 101:     const today = new Date()
 102:     const y = today.getFullYear()
 103:     const m = String(today.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0')
 104:     const d = String(today.getDate()).padStart(2, '0')
 105:     return new Date(`${y}-${m}-${d}T${timeValue}:00`)
 106:   }
 107:   return new Date()
 108: }
 109: 
 110: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 111: // Template resolution
 112: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 113: 
 114: export function resolveTemplateDir(skillRoot: string, root: string, localeValue: string): string {
 115:   const templatesRoot = join(skillRoot, 'assets', 'templates')
 116:   const localeName = normalizeLocale(localeValue, root)
 117:   const templateDir = join(templatesRoot, localeName)
 118:   if (!existsSync(templateDir)) {
 119:     throw new Error(`unsupported locale: ${localeValue}`)
 120:   }
 121:   return templateDir
 122: }
 123: 
 124: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 125: // Policy detection
 126: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 127: 
 128: export function findExternalPolicySources(root: string): string[] {
 129:   const matches = new Set<string>()
 130: 
 131:   for (const pattern of ALWAYS_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS) {
 132:     const p = join(root, pattern)
 133:     if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p)) {
 134:       matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))
 135:     }
 136:   }
 137: 
 138:   for (const pattern of CONTENT_POLICY_SOURCE_PATTERNS) {
 139:     const p = join(root, pattern)
 140:     if (existsSync(p) && isFile(p) && hasPolicyContent(p)) {
 141:       matches.add(toPosixPath(relative(root, p)))
 142:     }
 143:   }
 144: 
 145:   return [...matches].sort()
 146: }
 147: 
 148: export function hasPolicyContent(filePath: string): boolean {
 149:   let text: string
 150:   try {
 151:     text = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8').toLowerCase()
 152:   } catch {
 153:     return true
 154:   }
 155:   return POLICY_TEXT_PATTERNS.some(pattern => pattern.test(text))
 156: }
 157: 
 158: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 159: // Version detection
 160: // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 161: 
 162: export function findLatestVersion(folder: string, pattern: RegExp): string | null {
 163:   if (!existsSync(folder)) return null
 164:   const versions: string[] = []
 165:   try {
 166:     for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {
 167:       const match = pattern.exec(entry)
 168:       if (match?.[1]) {
 169:         versions.push(match[1])
 170:       }
 171:     }
 172:   } catch {
 173:     return null
 174:   }
 175:   return versions.length > 0 ? versions.sort().pop()! : null
 176: }
 177: 
 178: export function findLatestGoalFile(folder: string): string | null {
 179:   const regex = /^goal_v(\d{3})_(\d{8})\.md$/
 180:   const matches: Array<[string, string, string]> = []
 181:   if (!existsSync(folder)) return null
 182:   try {
 183:     for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {
 184:       const match = regex.exec(entry)
 185:       if (match) {
 186:         matches.push([match[1]!, match[2]!, join(folder, entry)])
 187:       }
 188:     }
 189:   } catch {
 190:     return null
 191:   }
 192:   if (matches.length === 0) return null
 193:   matches.sort()
 194:   return matches[matches.length - 1]![2]
 195: }
 196: 
 197: export function findLatestTodoVersion(folder: string, goalVersion: string): string | null {
 198:   const regex = new RegExp(`^todo_v${goalVersion}_(\\d{3})_\\d{8}\\.md$`)
 199:   const versions: string[] = []
 200:   if (!existsSync(folder)) return null
 201:   try {
 202:     for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {
 203:       const match = regex.exec(entry)
 204:       if (match?.[1]) versions.push(match[1])
 205:     }
 206:   } catch {
 207:     return null
 208:   }
 209:   return versions.length > 0 ? versions.sort().pop()! : null
 210: }
 211: 
 212: export function findLatestTodoFile(folder: string, goalVersion: string): string | null {
 213:   const regex = new RegExp(`^todo_v${goalVersion}_(\\d{3})_(\\d{8})\\.md$`)
 214:   const matches: Array<[string, string, string]> = []
 215:   if (!existsSync(folder)) return null
 216:   try {
 217:     for (const entry of readdirSync(folder)) {
 218:       const match = regex.exec(entry)
 219:       if (match) matches.push([match[1]!, match[2]!, join(folder, entry)])
 220:     }

Using Node v20.20.1
Using Node v20.20.1
   1: /**
   2:  * CLI: memorytree upgrade 鈥?upgrade a repo to MemoryTree safely.
   3:  * Port of scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py CLI wrapper.
   4:  */
   5: 
   6: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
   7: import { resolve } from 'node:path'
   8: 
   9: import { normalizeLocale } from '../project/locale.js'
  10: import { buildDatetime, resolveTemplateDir } from '../project/scaffold.js'
  11: import { upgrade, formatResultText } from '../project/upgrade.js'
  12: import { ensureManagedGitignore, type GitignoreEnsureResult } from '../project/scaffold.js'
  13: import { resolveSkillRoot } from '../utils/path.js'
  14: 
  15: export interface UpgradeOptions {
  16:   root: string
  17:   projectName: string
  18:   goalSummary: string
  19:   locale: string
  20:   date: string
  21:   time: string
  22:   format: string
  23: }
  24: 
  25: export function cmdUpgrade(options: UpgradeOptions): number {
  26:   const root = resolve(options.root)
  27:   if (!existsSync(root)) {
  28:     process.stderr.write(`root does not exist: ${root}\n`)
  29:     return 1
  30:   }
  31: 
  32:   const skillRoot = resolveSkillRoot(import.meta.url)
  33:   const templates = resolveTemplateDir(skillRoot, root, options.locale)
  34:   const effectiveLocale = normalizeLocale(options.locale, root)
  35:   const dt = buildDatetime(options.date, options.time)
  36: 
  37:   const result = upgrade(
  38:     root,
  39:     skillRoot,
  40:     templates,
  41:     effectiveLocale,
  42:     options.locale,
  43:     options.goalSummary,
  44:     options.projectName,
  45:     dt,
  46:   )
  47:   const gitignoreResult = ensureManagedGitignore(root)
  48: 
  49:   if (options.format === 'json') {
  50:     process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(result) + '\n')
  51:     writeHeartbeatNextStep(root, gitignoreResult, process.stderr)
  52:   } else {
  53:     process.stdout.write(formatResultText(result) + '\n')
  54:     writeHeartbeatNextStep(root, gitignoreResult)
  55:   }
  56:   return 0
  57: }
  58: 
  59: function writeHeartbeatNextStep(
  60:   root: string,
  61:   gitignoreResult: GitignoreEnsureResult | null,
  62:   stream: Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'> = process.stdout,
  63: ): void {
  64:   const displayRoot = root.includes(' ') ? `"${root}"` : root
  65:   stream.write('This command updated repository files only.\n')
  66:   writeGitignoreStatus(stream, gitignoreResult)
  67:   stream.write('It did not register heartbeat or modify ~/.memorytree/config.toml.\n')
  68:   stream.write('If you want the default heartbeat setup for this repository, run:\n')
  69:   stream.write(`  memorytree daemon quick-start --root ${displayRoot}\n`)
  70:   stream.write(
  71:     'Heartbeat keeps the dedicated MemoryTree branch as the shared source of truth ' +
  72:     'and refreshes this repository directory as a local cache mirror.\n',
  73:   )
  74: }
  75: 
  76: function writeGitignoreStatus(
  77:   stream: Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'>,
  78:   result: GitignoreEnsureResult | null,
  79: ): void {
  80:   if (result === null) return
  81:   if (result.changed) {
  82:     stream.write(`.gitignore updated: ${result.added.join(', ')}\n`)
  83:     return
  84:   }
  85:   stream.write('.gitignore already contains managed MemoryTree entries.\n')
  86: }

Private reasoning captured. Raw content is encrypted and not displayed.

assistant #391 commentary 05:03:56

新的失败点也很清楚了：这条 helper 在有些场景下其实“已经是 de-tracked 状态”，所以根本没有东西可提交。这个也应该被视为成功，而不是报错。我把 helper 再收一下，让它只在确实有变更时才 commit。

assistant #399 commentary 05:04:04

我再补一个小防抖：helper 在 git add .gitignore 后先看一次 status --short，如果已经没有变更，就直接返回，不再强行 commit。

Tool Call #400 apply_patch call_799YLL1oWQ8ambY07eY33Naf 05:04:09
apply_patch input=*** Begin Patch *** Update File: d:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\e2e\cli-workflow.test.ts @@ writeFileSync(gitignorePath, `${lines.join('\n')}\n`, 'utf-8') assertSuccess(runG...
Show input payload 
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: d:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\e2e\cli-workflow.test.ts
@@
   writeFileSync(gitignorePath, `${lines.join('\n')}\n`, 'utf-8')
   assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', '--ignore-unmatch', 'AGENTS.md'], root), 'git rm --cached AGENTS.md')
   assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', '--ignore-unmatch', '-r', 'Memory'], root), 'git rm --cached Memory')
   assertSuccess(runGit(['add', '.gitignore'], root), 'git add .gitignore for managed de-track')
+  const pending = runGit(['status', '--short'], root)
+  assertSuccess(pending, 'git status after managed de-track')
+  if (!pending.stdout.trim()) {
+    return
+  }
   assertSuccess(runGit(['commit', '-m', 'chore: de-track managed cache'], root), 'git commit managed de-track')
 }
*** End Patch

 [32m✓[39m tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts [2m([22m[2m24 tests[22m[2m)[22m[33m 21581[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mimports a transcript and builds the HTML report through the built CLI [33m 669[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mdiscovers current-project and all-projects transcripts through the built CLI [33m 892[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mrecalls the latest prior session and returns clean transcript content [33m 483[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mserves the generated report over real HTTP [33m 802[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22muses report_port from global config when report serve omits --port [33m 798[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mregisters a repository with a dedicated heartbeat worktree through the CLI [33m 829[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mallows overriding the memory branch name in detailed register settings [33m 542[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps existing register settings when quick-start is rerun for the same repository [33m 836[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mruns heartbeat through a registered worktree and syncs outputs back to the development directory [33m 1820[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps a de-tracked development branch clean while heartbeat refreshes cache mirrors from the worktree [33m 2037[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mcreates a snapshot commit when no new transcripts are imported but context changes [33m 2480[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mruns heartbeat end-to-end on a dedicated memorytree branch and commits imports [33m 1244[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps repo raw transcript mirrors unstaged when raw upload permission is not approved [33m 1165[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mcommits repo raw transcript mirrors when raw upload permission is approved [33m 1124[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mcommits managed content on a memorytree branch even after AGENTS.md and Memory/** are ignored [33m 1492[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps repository branches clean during heartbeat on non-memorytree branches [33m 576[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mdeploys heartbeat reports to a gh-pages branch with CNAME [33m 1843[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps heartbeat successful when webhook delivery is rejected [33m 1026[2mms[22m[39m

+  if (!pending.stdout.trim()) {
+    return
+  }
   assertSuccess(runGit(['commit', '-m', 'chore: de-track managed cache'], root), 'git commit managed de-track')
 }

   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mrecalls the latest prior session and returns clean transcript content [33m 489[2mms[22m[39m

   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22muses report_port from global config when report serve omits --port [33m 691[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mregisters a repository with a dedicated heartbeat worktree through the CLI [33m 897[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mallows overriding the memory branch name in detailed register settings [33m 518[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps existing register settings when quick-start is rerun for the same repository [33m 802[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mruns heartbeat through a registered worktree and syncs outputs back to the development directory [33m 1546[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mkeeps a de-tracked development branch clean while heartbeat refreshes cache mirrors from the worktree [33m 1837[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mcreates a snapshot commit when no new transcripts are imported but context changes [33m 2429[2mms[22m[39m
   [33m[2m✓[22m[39m CLI E2E[2m > [22mruns heartbeat end-to-end on a dedicated memorytree branch and commits imports [33m 1104[2mms[22m[39m

index 3631ff0..00ee854 100644
--- a/tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts
+++ b/tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts
@@ -1192,9 +1192,14 @@ function detrackManagedCache(root: string): void {
   }
 
   writeFileSync(gitignorePath, `${lines.join('\n')}\n`, 'utf-8')
-  assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', 'AGENTS.md'], root), 'git rm --cached AGENTS.md')
-  assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', '-r', 'Memory'], root), 'git rm --cached Memory')
+  assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', '--ignore-unmatch', 'AGENTS.md'], root), 'git rm --cached AGENTS.md')
+  assertSuccess(runGit(['rm', '--cached', '--ignore-unmatch', '-r', 'Memory'], root), 'git rm --cached Memory')
   assertSuccess(runGit(['add', '.gitignore'], root), 'git add .gitignore for managed de-track')
+  const pending = runGit(['status', '--short'], root)
+  assertSuccess(pending, 'git status after managed de-track')
+  if (!pending.stdout.trim()) {
+    return
+  }
   assertSuccess(runGit(['commit', '-m', 'chore: de-track managed cache'], root), 'git commit managed de-track')
 }

assistant #538 final_answer 05:10:55

E2E 问题已经修好并合并了。修复点在 [tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts#L1180)：detrackManagedCache() 现在会对 git rm --cached 使用 --ignore-unmatch，并且在实际上没有变更时跳过空提交，所以不会再因为 AGENTS.md 已经未跟踪或“nothing to commit”而把 E2E 打红。

我把修复补到了 PR #39 分支，重新跑完 checks 后已经合并。PR 链接是 https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39 ，merge commit 是 934859a515e645ca7a961577170010ecb7109e02。本地 main 也已经快进到同一个提交，和 origin/main 一致。

验证上，我本地跑了整套 npm run test:e2e，24/24 通过；GitHub 上这次 PR 的 E2E 和 CI 也都转绿后才合并。当前工作树里还剩几个未跟踪的本地文件：.claude/、一个 docs/ 里的未跟踪文档、还有 ~/，这些我没有动。

Show task summary

E2E 问题已经修好并合并了。修复点在 [tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts](D:/demo1/memorytree-workflow/tests/e2e/cli-workflow.test.ts#L1180)：detrackManagedCache() 现在会对 git rm --cached 使用 --ignore-unmatch，并且在实际上没有变更时跳过空提交，所以不会再因为 AGENTS.md 已经未跟踪或“nothing to commit”而把 E2E 打红。

我把修复补到了 PR #39 分支，重新跑完 checks 后已经合并。PR 链接是 https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow/pull/39 ，merge commit 是 934859a515e645ca7a961577170010ecb7109e02。本地 main 也已经快进到同一个提交，和 origin/main 一致。

验证上，我本地跑了整套 npm run test:e2e，24/24 通过；GitHub 上这次 PR 的 E2E 和 CI 也都转绿后才合并。当前工作树里还剩几个未跟踪的本地文件：.claude/、一个 docs/ 里的未跟踪文档、还有 ~/，这些我没有动。

  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients, and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",

    "limit": 10000
  }
